description_long: This is a rectangular cowshed with a gable roof covered in reddish-brown shingles, giving it a rustic appearance. The walls are constructed from gray concrete, providing a sturdy and utilitarian look. On one long side, there is a small rectangular window and an external air conditioning unit, along with a utility box and visible piping. The opposite long side features another small window and a long wooden or metal rail running along the base. The front of the building has two large, dark, barn-style doors, likely for animal or equipment access, and a small handle or latch is visible. The rear side is plain, with only some exposed piping running vertically and horizontally along the wall. The overall design is functional, with minimal decorative elements, suitable for agricultural or semi-industrial urban edge environments.
description: A sturdy, rectangular cowshed with a reddish-brown gable roof, gray concrete walls, and large barn doors. Features include small windows, utility boxes, and exposed piping for a utilitarian look.
description_view_0: Side view showing a small window, an air conditioning unit, a utility box, and some piping on a plain concrete wall.
description_view_1: Front view with two large barn-style doors, a small handle, and the gable roof peak above.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with a small window and a long rail or pipe running along the base of the wall.
description_view_3: Rear view with plain concrete wall and exposed vertical and horizontal piping, no doors or windows.
attribute_door_style: double sliding barn doors, dark metal or wood construction, utilitarian design
attribute_window_presence: two small rectangular windows, one on each long side
attribute_ventilation_features: mechanical fan (air conditioning unit) and possible vented eaves
